meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Debugging with AI to Solve Coding Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Learn how to master debugging with AI and overcome coding roadblocks. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first projects.
content.answer_block: Stop spinning your wheels on syntax errors. By integrating Antigravity for architectural visualization and Claude Code for automated iterative fixes, you transform debugging from a frustrating chore into a high-speed learning engine. This workflow allows you to diagnose complex bugs in seconds, accelerating your development cycle and building professional-grade confidence.
content.intro:
The screen is a blur of red text. You’ve spent three hours chasing a single state-management bug, and your motivation is cratering. You aren't just stuck; you're trapped in the 'beginner's plateau,' where the gap between your vision and your execution feels like an impassable canyon. 

But here is the secret: senior engineers don't debug harder; they debug smarter. By leveraging AI-native tools, you can offload the cognitive load of syntax and logic tracing, allowing you to focus on the high-level architecture. This is how you stop being a student who struggles and start being a developer who ships.

steps.title: Define the Failure State
steps.description: Before fixing, you must articulate the bug clearly. Use Antigravity to map the expected flow versus the actual output to isolate the failing component.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided codebase and map the data flow for the authentication component. Identify where the state transition fails when the user clicks 'submit'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Explain the current state transition logic in auth.js'

steps.title: Isolate the Logic Gap
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a trace of the execution path. This ensures you aren't just guessing, but following the logical thread of the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Visualize the dependency tree for the database connection module and highlight potential null reference triggers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Trace the execution path of the submit handler and identify the null value source.'

steps.title: Generate the Fix Strategy
steps.description: Don't just paste code. Ask the AI to propose three different architectural solutions so you can compare their efficiency and side effects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare three ways to refactor this async function to handle edge-case errors without increasing latency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the handleAuth function using the most robust error handling pattern.'

steps.title: Validate with Unit Tests
steps.description: Before applying the fix, generate a test case that specifically targets the bug. If the test fails, your fix is not yet complete.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Jest test suite that replicates the current crash scenario.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a unit test in auth.test.js that asserts the failure condition.'

steps.title: Deploy and Document
steps.description: Apply the fix, run the suite, and use AI to document the 'why' behind the change, building your professional portfolio documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the bug fix and the architectural improvement in a professional commit message format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the fix to auth.js and verify all tests pass.'

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind a fix, not just the 'what'. This turns every bug into a learning opportunity.
Use AI to generate JSDoc comments for your functions; it forces you to clarify your logic before the AI writes the code.
When stuck, ask the AI to play 'Rubber Duck'—explain your logic to it and have it point out the logical fallacies.
Treat your AI interactions as a code review process; never accept the first output without verifying it against your project constraints.
Keep a 'Bug Log' in a Notion doc where you paste the AI's explanation of your solved bugs; this becomes your ultimate interview prep guide.
content.next_steps: After mastering this debugging loop, apply it to your next feature request. Move from fixing bugs to building modular, reusable components.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact workflow used by top-tier engineers to ship faster and learn deeper. Don't let your next bug stop your momentum. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
